rulings.Case Title: MICKEY Y WONG VS. DORIS WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Doris Wongs Notice Of Motion Motion For An Order Barring Plaintiff From Offering Any Documents At Trial Other Than Documents Previously Produced Because Of His Disobedience Of Court Order; Request For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 28, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT DORIS WONG's Motion For An Order Barring Plaintiff From Offering Any Documents At Trial Other Than Documents Previously Produced Because Of His Disobedience Of Court Order; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This matter will be dropped from the Department 302 Discovery docket. The motion is presently set for hearing on May 13, 2015 in Department 501 of this Court. The parties should deliver new courtesy copies of all motion pleadings to the Clerk of Department 501 as it appears the pleadings delivered to Department 302 are incomplete. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON BROWN VS. PG&E CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication is denied. Defendant has maintained its initial burden of production as to the 3 retaliation causes of action. The declarations of the decisionmakers (Browne dec., par. 22; Jean dec., par.8; and Yamaguvchi dec., par. 3) show that the discharge was based on legitimate performance issues. In opposition, Plaintiff has presented substantial responsive evidence creating a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the proffered business reason for the discharge was pretextual. See Hersant v. Dept. of Social Services, 57 Cal.App.4th 997. First, Browne was one of the decisionmakers and he clearly had notice of Plaintiff's complaints about discriminatory treatment. Second, Plaintiff's declaration shows that Browne's conduct toward Plaintiff changed after the complaints. Third, Plaintiff's prior unblemished 20-year work history and positive evaluations in 2009 and 2010 (Browne completed the 2010 evaluation) are probative and show that Defendant's claimed reason for the termination is unworthy of credence. Fourth, the declarations of co-workers Wiley, Towne, and Tamayo show that profanity is routinely used at the workplace and Defendant may have overblown Plaintiff's use of such language as a reason for the termination. The court rejects Defendant's reply argument that Mr. Kenyon was the person that was really responsible for the decision to terminate Plaintiff's employment. Defendant's undisputed fact number 22 and paragraph 23 of Mr. Browne's declaration indicates that Mr. Browne had a role in the decisionmaking process. The motion is denied as moot as to the remaining claims, which were dismissed on April 7, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KEN LAM et al VS. CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant California Capital Insurance Company'S Motion To Compel Answers To Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel In The Amount Of $2,875
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 28, 2015, Line 7, Defendant California Capital Insurance Company's Motion To Compel Answers To Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel In The Amount Of $2,875. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Plaintiffs' motion to compel is granted. The special interrogatories may be technically compound in their precise language. But they each reflect an inquiry to a single subject. The mandate of Clement v. Alegre, 177 Cal. App. 4th 1277 (2009) applies to this discovery dispute. To the extent plaintiffs believe they are unable to respond to any individual special interrogatory the Court will review same at the hearing and in its discretion will reformulate the interrogatory language. Plaintiffs' objection the motion was filed after the agreed date for filing is overruled. Plaintiffs' timely service of the motion and timely attempt to file same with the Clerk of the Court who initially rejected filing of the pleadings based on a Local Rule which conflicts with California Rule of Court 3.1112, is not a basis for the Court to not consider the motion on the merits. Monetary sanctions in the sum of $2,875.00 are awarded to the defendant pursuant to CCP 2023.030 to be paid within 25 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 2626 LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. COVE CONSTRUCTION, A CALIFORNIA PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants To Enforce Settlement And Enter Judgment Under Code Of Civil Procedure Â§ 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANTS COVE CONSTRUCTION, ROBERT CAIN, AND MICHAEL TEAGUE'S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants To Enforce Settlement And Enter Judgment Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sec. 664.6. The court grants defendants' motion and orders a judgment of dismissal to be entered. The settlement does not have a certain date when the payment was due. Therefore, the payment was due within a reasonable time. See Estate of Baird (1943) 59 Cal.App.2d 303, 306. Defendants made the payment within a reasonable time and Plaintiff accepted that payment. Paragraph 5 of the settlement provides that if the sale that was contemplated in early 2014 flounders, then Defendants will execute a deed of trust and rent assignment on the property in Plaintiff's favor by February 28, 2014. The settlement does not state that the payment must be made by that date. In addition, even if the settlement had a certain date and time was of the essence, Plaintiff's acceptance of the payment constitutes a waiver of that provision. See Galdjie v. Darwish (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 1331, 1339. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EAST WEST BANK VS. AMIR SHAHMIRZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Dismiss Cross-Complaints First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015, Line 7, CROSS-DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S Motion To Dismiss Cross-Complaints First Amended Complaint. Denied. The Settlement Agreement is admissible pursuant to Cal. Evid. Code sec. 1123 because paragraph nine and the fact that all parties executed the agreement evidences the parties' intent to be bound. (See In re Marriage of Daly and Oyster (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 505, 510.) The motion is denied because there remains a dispute about when the cross-complaint should be dismissed. The email from the mediator is too vague to be a dispositive determination of the issue. For example, is the mediator referring to the signed handwritten agreement or a signed "more formally draft[ed] version," as provided for in paragraph nine? Pursuant to paragraph nine, the mediator must decide the issue of when the cross-complaint must be dismissed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRIS MIORELLI et al VS. WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015, Line 12, PLAINTIFFS CHRIS MIORELLI AND TRINA USA LLC'S Motion For Reconsideration. The court grants the motion to vacate the demurrer order entered on February 3, 2015, pursuant to CCP 473(b). There is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. Plaintiff demonstrates excusable neglect and the court gives plaintiff leave to file the amended complaint. Plaintiff must file the amended complaint by April 30, 2015. Plaintiff's counsel shall remit $2,000 to defendant by May 8, 2015. See Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822-823. Plaintiff's failure to file a timely and proper opposition to the demurrer, failure to comply with the court's rules regarding contesting a tentative ruling, etc. caused defendant to incur the fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel production of documents is unopposed and granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. A verified response to defendant's document request and all documents responsive to the request shall be served within 20 days of service of a copy of this order with notice of entry.
